mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue beats and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with rising industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply expansion, which compresses input cost inflation while boosting earnings momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in at least two consecutive calendar years, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a sharp contraction in real consumer credit signals tightening financial conditions that choke corporate liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or RSAFS declines do not correlate with subsequent earnings downgrades in S&P 500 constituents.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate confirms robust economic expansion, which historically supports corporate profit growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month following the data release.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of post-release windows, or the cross-sectional correlation between GDPC1 surprises and SPX returns falls below 0.05 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.0 percent year-over-year, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where CPI growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% after adjusting for FEDFUNDS trajectory.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth paired with declining unemployment creates a wage-price spiral that squeezes corporate margins, prompting equity devaluation and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least two consecutive calendar years, or PAYEMS and UNRATE move inversely without producing a statistically significant interaction effect on forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruptions, a sustained drop in industrial production below 100.0 indicates capacity constraints that trigger cost-push inflation and earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 98.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or INDPRO declines do not precede measurable increases in input cost indices or margin compression in S&P 500 earnings reports.
